tweets.text: A recent report from @SmithsonianCRI identifies over 450 potential impacts to cultural heritage sites across Ukraine from February to June 2022 at the hands of Russia's forces, including archaeological sites, museums, and places of worship. More: https://t.co/mxPVhgson8 https://t.co/jdPlXZPSsZ

In the latest article from @ShareAmerica, learn how the United States is providing high-quality seeds to wheat farmers in northeast Syria to help feed people during the global food security crisis. @USAID @USAIDSavesLives  Full story: https://t.co/4kU2DDhG8I

.@SecBlinken: Widespread hunger makes communities and countries less secure, particularly now when food insecurity around the world is on the rise because of Russia’s war of aggression, which has blocked millions of tons of Ukrainian crops from being shipped to global markets. https://t.co/18XI21nQF5
.@SecBlinken: The United States will not dictate Africa’s choices, and neither should anyone else. The right to make these choices belongs to Africans, and Africans alone. https://t.co/IHP1xFkNKH

On this second @UN International Day for People of #AfricanDescent, the U.S. celebrates the contributions of persons of African descent to our communities and society. We are committed to promoting racial equity and justice both at home and around the world. https://t.co/Nj6ntaUNaL

Deputy @AsstSecStateAF Akunna Cook and Chief Operating Officer for @ProsperAfricaUS Leslie Marbury discuss working to build a more prosperous Africa through government initiatives. #ProsperAfrica Visit https://t.co/Ui8aaFzVRY for more. https://t.co/KDDHXF0B2f

At 9:30 a.m. EDT today, join @USAID Administrator @PowerUSAID for a discussion on how the United States and partners are responding to the global hunger crisis.  Visit https://t.co/22Wtw07SD0 to learn more.

Last week, the United States partnered with #COVAX to donate 8 million pediatric COVID-19 vaccine doses and over 1 million adult COVID-19 vaccine doses to the people of Pakistan. #USPAK continues to work together to ensure safe and effective vaccines are widely available. https://t.co/Q4XdEn2Wij

The U.S. welcomes the arrival of Ukrainian grain for Ethiopia and Somalia today via the ship Brave Commander. This is among the first Ukrainian grain transported via the Black Sea to reach those most in need since President Putin’s full-scale attack on the country in February.

In today's briefing, @StateDeputySpox announced $30 million in humanitarian assistance through @USAID to support those affected by flooding in Pakistan and outlined the Kremlin's preparations to stage sham referenda in areas of Ukraine under its control. https://t.co/WTD5lsilwL

.@SecBlinken: @POTUS has committed to putting human rights back at the center of our foreign policy, and that’s a commitment I and the entire Department of State take very seriously. https://t.co/qpfSceNQ15

.@SecBlinken: “Governments that violate human rights are almost always the same ones that flout other key parts of that order – such as invading, coercing, and threatening other countries, or breaking trade rules.” The U.S. will continue to take a stand for human rights for all. https://t.co/o5YlTs3iKy

On this International Day of the Victims of Enforced Disappearances, we call on governments around the world to put an end to this practice, hold those responsible to account, and respect the human rights and fundamental freedoms of all persons. Statement: https://t.co/BxIlUiJuOg https://t.co/8xR9odYB85

As @VP noted at the launch of the White House Action Plan on Global Water Security, more than two billion people lack access to safely managed drinking water. We must take action to make the world more water-secure. #WorldWaterWeek https://t.co/teRIABK2L2 https://t.co/jPU9al4SUj

In today’s briefing, @StateDeputySpox addressed the situation in Iraq: “We’re aware of the reports of increasing violence and potential casualties, and we condemn the use of violence. Now is the time for dialogue.” More in the full transcript: https://t.co/9pzVScNuHS

Globally, women are less likely to benefit from agroforestry due to lack of land ownership and exclusion from land management decisions. But we know that integrating women into agroforestry programs is beneficial for everyone—our planet included. More: https://t.co/ml7a2Z0X6C
Sign up to receive "From the Desk of Secretary of State Antony J. Blinken," email updates from the Secretary on key foreign affairs issues. https://t.co/Rgm7xA0z8l https://t.co/oHEhWkU87w
.@POTUS: America is an idea – unique in the world. We are all created equal. It’s who we are. We cannot walk away from that principle. https://t.co/rn8uEb4lpk

Today in 1963, Martin Luther King Jr. delivered his "I Have a Dream" speech at the March on Washington. He spoke on civil and economic rights, calling for an end to racism. We continue to work toward racial equity and an America and world where all can reach their full potential. https://t.co/eJwrXzNlIl
This week @USAmbUN talked with the @ChicagoCouncil about the global food security crisis and what the United States is doing to address it. More: https://t.co/M9p9dHsR9R https://t.co/MeRjt6cKoQ

This week, we marked six months since the Kremlin launched a premeditated and full-scale war of aggression against its sovereign neighbor, Ukraine. The world has been awed by Ukrainian strength and courage. Today and every day, we are #UnitedWithUkraine https://t.co/lTwGNqTMkz https://t.co/0dRi5LRbAJ

In "The Week at State," @SecBlinken addresses the Crimea Platform Summit, as the world marks six months since Russia's full-scale invasion of Ukraine. For the latest, visit https://t.co/6RJHUnp5W8 and subscribe to our newsletter at https://t.co/2sMdDfZOh7. https://t.co/ewXz11nEAG
.@USAmbUN: The Biden Administration has been using every diplomatic tool in our arsenal to tackle the food insecurity crisis head-on. https://t.co/N6mdiX8ORr

Today, in partnership with #COVAX, the United States delivered an additional 4,680 COVID-19 vaccine doses to Saint Vincent and the Grenadines. The United States has donated a total of nearly 115,000 vaccine doses to help Vincentians fight the COVID-19 pandemic. https://t.co/X37KvQrKYj

.@SciDiplomacyUSA’s Assistant Secretary Medina traveled to the @UN this week in support of negotiations on the “High Seas Treaty.” Read her latest blog to learn 5 reasons why this agreement matters. https://t.co/HN1AoackbT https://t.co/knRZHOsxJQ

.@SecBlinken: When we design our foreign policy with the rights and needs of women and girls in mind, our policy is more effective, more humane, and more likely to make a lasting difference in people’s lives. https://t.co/AWRz4n9Vlf

As we commemorate 200+ years of continued advancement toward gender equity and equality, we look at the strides taken through our Women, Peace, and Security efforts over the past few years. Read more about it in DipNote: https://t.co/QowC80fY0a.
.@VP Harris: American Aspiration is what drove the women of this nation, throughout history, to demand equal rights. https://t.co/5K9dizUCuZ

Today on Women’s Equality Day, we celebrate the achievements of women’s rights activists and leaders in the United States and around the world. We are committed to a future where all people can fully participate in society. Women’s rights are human rights. https://t.co/YXjK48gNqx

President Putin and his government will not be able to engage in human rights abuses and atrocities with impunity. Accountability is imperative, and the United States and our partners will not be silent. More: https://t.co/pO7wAFx2n6 https://t.co/NHy1rRyQQQ

Today’s @HRL_YaleSPH report documents 21 known sites where Russia’s forces are conducting filtration operations. Visit https://t.co/HLLWj2rtQX to read the full report. https://t.co/pO7wAFx2n6 https://t.co/xpzXwRNnl9

In a @ForeignPressCtr briefing, Richard Nephew shares more about his mandate as Coordinator on Global Anti-Corruption and highlights how the Department is working with international partners to advance U.S. anti-corruption policy around the world: https://t.co/VsgO87zKhd

Today @StateDeputySpox announced $9M in additional funds for @ObserveConflict's work in reporting & documenting the Kremlin’s atrocities. “President Putin and all those who commit heinous acts must be held to account for violations of international law.” https://t.co/MMXX1mFCkN

Coordinated efforts to counter corruption stemming from the Kremlin and elsewhere is a top priority for the United States. Learn more about @StateDeptPM's strategy to fight corruption, enhance transparency, and promote good practices: https://t.co/pzKi2a117A

A new Department-supported #ConflictObservatory report includes multiple pieces of evidence of Russia-perpetrated filtration operations in, and deportations from, Ukraine. These operations are tearing families apart. https://t.co/pO7wAFx2n6

.@NatlParkService has worked to preserve the natural and cultural resources of America’s public lands since 1916. Today we celebrate the inspiration and enjoyment that our national parks bring to Americans and visitors from around the world. https://t.co/9NPw1hB40h

At a time when it is unsafe for Ukrainian youth to return home due to the Russian government's unprovoked war, Camps Kenwood & Evergreen and @acacamps have helped provide a degree of normalcy for @ecaatstate's BridgeUSA & @flexprogram students in the United States. #SlavaUkraini https://t.co/EGUj2Q4wEq

Beijing floods social media with conspiracy theories, propaganda, and disinformation to bury the facts about its atrocities and human rights abuses, which impact Uyghurs around the world. Read how and why: https://t.co/2hQcBYQuiB

This week, the United States, in collaboration with #COVAX, donated nearly 10 million additional COVID-19 vaccine doses to the people of Bangladesh. We are proud to partner with Bangladesh to help expand safe and effective vaccine access to people who need them most. https://t.co/nDgfaKYBDv

.@StateDeputySpox in today's briefing: "The United States will continue to stand #UnitedWithUkraine and help it defend itself for as long as it takes." Read the full transcript: https://t.co/u6hRhoiZY6
It has been six months since Russia launched its full-scale invasion of Ukraine. Learn more about Russia's attempts to use disinformation to legitimize its bloody war: https://t.co/v9xooaErne

Today Ukrainians are celebrating their national independence day — just as Russia’s full-scale invasion of their country reaches a grim six-month milestone. @ShareAmerica provides a timeline of Putin’s war: https://t.co/ini6z4H4oI

Six months into Russia's invasion of Ukraine, the Kremlin continues to spread lies in an effort to hide the truth about their actions. We're fighting back. Follow @TheGEC to learn more about how the United States is countering foreign disinformation and propaganda.

Six months ago, on February 24, the Kremlin launched a premeditated and full-scale war of aggression against its sovereign neighbor, Ukraine. The world has been awed by Ukrainian strength and courage. Today and every day, we are #UnitedWithUkraine https://t.co/lTwGNrbnJ9 https://t.co/cJjsx8Ah57

On Ukraine’s National Day, we stand #UnitedWithUkraine in protection of their sovereignty, democracy, independence, and territorial integrity. The brave people of Ukraine have stood strong in the face of six months of Russian aggression. Slava Ukraini

У День Незалежності України ми стоїмо #UnitedWithUkraine на захисті їхнього суверенітету, демократії, незалежності та територіальної цілісності. Мужній народ України вистояв протягом шести місяців російської агресії. Слава Україні! https://t.co/vNYngKs3T3

Ukraine’s distinct culture, national identity, & language have existed for centuries, and Putin’s cynical attempts to spread disinformation about Ukraine's independence as a sovereign state intentionally manipulate the facts. Learn more: https://t.co/m2oxJZwpSN
For six months, global citizens in cities across the world have spoken out against Russian aggression, showing unity and support for the people of Ukraine. #UnitedWithUkraine https://t.co/q9hYJ6qdZA

As we commemorate 200+ years of continued advancement toward gender equity and equality, we look at the strides taken through our Women, Peace, and Security efforts over the past few years. Read more about it in DipNote: https://t.co/r6gju2Y7dV

The Kremlin creates and spreads disinformation in an attempt to confuse and overwhelm people about Russia’s real actions in Ukraine, Georgia, and elsewhere in Europe. Disarming disinformation is our shared responsibility. https://t.co/LsNIkO2l3a
The United States is leading the way to help those who face hunger and malnutrition, while also working hard to address the root causes of the food security crisis. https://t.co/QsXN0bJJmu

We are committed to leading an international and coordinated vaccine effort. The United States, through #COVAX, donated nearly 123,000 additional doses of the COVID-19 vaccine to the people of Tajikistan. We are proud to work together to bring an end to COVID-19. https://t.co/BoiIpwMUnR

The U.S. and international sanctions imposed after Russia's full invasion of Ukraine are having a powerful impact on Russia's economy. The world will not be fooled by the Kremlin's attempts to hide the facts by spreading disinformation. Learn more here: https://t.co/wU16pPJuAI

Last week, the United States delivered 35,100 COVID-19 vaccine doses to the people of the Solomon Islands. We are proud to partner with #COVAX in supporting the Solomon Islands’ fight against COVID-19 and remain committed to ending the acute phase of the pandemic. https://t.co/QihzMmASaf
.@POTUS: From President Zelenskyy to every Ukrainian, their fearlessness, their courage, their determination, inspires the world. https://t.co/AgE5NZMlKA

.@SecBlinken: Religious freedom is a key element of an open and stable society. Without it, people aren’t able to make their fullest contribution to their country’s success. And whenever human rights are denied, it ignites tension, it breeds division. https://t.co/5luzclM730
Live now! @StateDeptSpox briefs reporters from the State Department, also streaming on https://t.co/AOekWqtkK2. https://t.co/o9LvaRu8Bb

.@SecBlinken: Religious freedom, like every human right, is universal. All people, everywhere, are entitled to it no matter where they live, what they believe, or what they don’t believe. https://t.co/hOt8QHPKbh

.@POTUS: Thanks to the courage and bravery of the Ukrainian people, the equipment we’ve sent, and our colleagues have sent have been used to devastating effect to defend Ukrainian land and airspace. https://t.co/BJGpa78VvY

In "The Week of State," Secretary Blinken meets with representatives of the African diaspora and alumni of the Mandela Washington Fellowship. For the latest, visit https://t.co/58Uc9lZRv7. https://t.co/rBHLMNHZk1

.@State_E will travel to Charlotte, NC, to support the textile industry and highlight the co-production of textile goods between U.S. companies and businesses in northern Central America, which supports jobs in both the United States and the region. 
https://t.co/BEhTVmMQlX

.@POTUS: Putin’s assault on Ukraine is a challenge to the peace and stability everywhere in the world…The United States will continue to support Ukraine and the Ukrainian people who have been devastated and displaced by the Russian violence. #UnitedWithUkraine https://t.co/VuuL1tWjyV

Immunization is one of the most cost-effective public health interventions to stop the spread of preventable infectious diseases. Learn how @USAID's support for immunization contributes to our global health and broader development objectives. #VaccinesWork https://t.co/En3txVPGho

#WorldMosquitoDay marks the anniversary of the discovery that mosquitoes transmit malaria. Malaria kills more than 625,000 people each year — that’s more than 1 death per minute. Learn more from @CDCgov and read about our Malaria Initiative @PMIgov: https://t.co/hHff0UXnfp https://t.co/hF0nLwOlDe

International collaboration is crucial for combating mosquito-borne diseases. @SciDiplomacyUSA is partnering with @GLOBEProgram on #GLOBEZika to engage citizen scientists in 30+ countries on disease prevention and mosquito surveillance. #WorldMosquitoDay https://t.co/g28P4K25Dk https://t.co/Rgo3HvjVDd

.@SecBlinken: COVID-19 has taught us that when it comes to global health, none of us are safe until all of us are safe — and that our health security is closely interlinked with our economic and national security. We must not waste this hard-earned knowledge. https://t.co/BMlremM1Al

As long as the virus is spreading anywhere, it’s a threat to people everywhere—including Americans at home. That’s why the United States will share over 1.2 billion COVID-19 vaccine doses with countries around the world. Track our progress and learn more: https://t.co/dyUVHcVPnx

In the latest DipNote blog from @SciDiplomacyUSA's Assistant Secretary Medina, learn more about our once-in-a-lifetime opportunity to advance down a more sustainable path that values and conserves nature. https://t.co/k0hx0Pr60r

All species of orangutans are critically endangered. The leading threats are habitat destruction and trafficking for bushmeat — and humans who want to use them as pets. This #WorldOrangutanDay, learn more about what we're doing to #EndWildlifeTrafficking: https://t.co/kTfxZlVPQD https://t.co/m9qUUyBF8o

Around the world, #ItTakesAVillage of humanitarians to deliver life-saving assistance to vulnerable communities. Today on #WorldHumanitarianDay, and every day, we honor humanitarians who risk their lives to help those in need. https://t.co/eSD4eH1NTi

Live now! Briefing on the procurement of Ukrainian wheat to address acute food insecurity with @USAID  Deputy Administrator Isobel Coleman and the Head of the Office of Sanctions Coordination Ambassador Jim O’Brien @ForeignPressCtr https://t.co/PhHoxkQHgX

Bold action to tackle the climate crisis is needed now, more urgently than ever. Through our actions at home and our leadership abroad, we are doing our part to build a ​more sustainable future that creates good jobs and ​supports a healthy planet for future generations. https://t.co/dodUMQtffd

The cascading impacts of the climate crisis do not discriminate by nation or border. We all must follow through on existing commitments and make stronger and more urgent efforts to protect future generations from the dangers of climate change. Learn more: https://t.co/0ZDki6yFnJ

.@StateDeptSpox: The United States remains deeply concerned about Russia’s military takeover and continued control of the Zaporizhzhia Nuclear Power Plant. @iaeaorg must be given access to ZNPP as soon as possible to help ensure the safety and security of the plant. https://t.co/mZIPDBGyvx
Live now! @StateDeptSpox briefs reporters from the State Department, also streaming on https://t.co/WmSHKMwZnL. https://t.co/hRgea4Kbj2

America is leading the world on addressing the climate crisis and we will continue on this path. If we succeed, we will capitalize on the greatest opportunity to create quality jobs in generations, build a more equitable, healthy, and sustainable society, and protect our planet. https://t.co/NGpl7xgFPv

The program honors the legacy of Colin Powell, who dedicated his extraordinary life to public service, including as the first African-American Secretary of State. Former Secretary Powell's 13 Rules of Leadership guide Department principles to this day: https://t.co/yBIhWINhzB

.@SecBlinken: “Next month we will launch the Colin Powell Leadership Program. This program will recruit college students and recent graduates to the civil service.” More on this @doscareers opportunity for college students at: https://t.co/wrCEqG4Dk7 https://t.co/hWEv9TmJ62

Don't miss your chance to learn more about the @RangelProgram, which prepares outstanding young people for careers as diplomats in the Foreign Service. Join the virtual info session on August 18: https://t.co/sFrMtxfiFM https://t.co/vmgEvMph3O

Use your passion and commitment to community service for global change. Become a 2023 Payne Fellow, which seeks outstanding individuals who are interested in pursuing careers in the Foreign Service of @USAID. Apply today at https://t.co/vouae30ufT. https://t.co/9LUpx4psNs
Live now! @StateDeptSpox briefs reporters from the State Department, also streaming on https://t.co/mZbL3Baeu4. https://t.co/MSKbKhgNx6

The Thomas R. Pickering Foreign Affairs Graduate Fellowship Program is now accepting applications for the 2023 Fellowship. The Fellowship values varied backgrounds and prepares outstanding young people for Foreign Service careers. Learn more: https://t.co/Tkci2kZXW7

Are you eager to put your tech talents to use for your country? Consider the @doscareers' Foreign Affairs Information Technology Fellowship, a unique public service opportunity where you can use your IT talent to facilitate interconnected diplomacy. https://t.co/bgTM0bElZN

Live now! @SecBlinken delivers remarks on the anniversaries of the Department of State’s Pickering, Rangel, Payne, and Foreign Affairs IT (FAIT) Fellowship Program. Also streaming on https://t.co/FqcRpLAXef. https://t.co/4CRFlPBH0f

Being a State Department intern or fellow is an exciting way to gain insights on foreign policy, explore different career paths, and pick up skills that can help represent America to the world. Learn more about opportunities to advance diplomacy worldwide: https://t.co/JAhzP1nxdd

Deputy @AsstSecStateAF Akunna Cook and Chief Operating Officer for @ProsperAfricaUS Leslie Marbury discuss working to build a more prosperous Africa through government initiatives. Visit https://t.co/Ui8aaFzVRY for more. #ProsperAfrica https://t.co/KlqbmZY7BL
The United States has provided $1 billion toward the $2 billion commitment to African countries for emergency food security assistance. Read more in the fact sheet: https://t.co/ToL9g1TzNz
Live now! @StateDeptSpox briefs reporters from the State Department, also streaming on https://t.co/Ve1ShVpktY. https://t.co/fcM3Et9TOk

The @iaeaorg's Peaceful Uses Initiative has helped advance health, water resource management, food security, sustainable development, and clean energy efforts around the globe. Learn more about our PUI contributions in @StateISN's latest DipNote blog: https://t.co/FOmoyKUqhX https://t.co/4s5jITR99X

To help combat the COVID-19 pandemic, the United States has provided more than 173 million COVID-19 vaccine doses to 44 countries in Sub-Saharan Africa. Read the latest @ShareAmerica article on increasing COVID-19 vaccinations in Africa. https://t.co/YDlMasg4ng

The U.S. recognizes that Sub-Saharan Africa plays a critical role in advancing global priorities to the benefit of Africans and Americans. That is why we are building on our actions and commitments to deepen our engagement and partnerships in Africa. More: https://t.co/DrIlMPnWGm https://t.co/lgzkeEALAk

.@StateDeptSpox: The 20-year-long U.S. military mission in Afghanistan ended nearly one year ago. As @SecBlinken has said, the end of our military mission in Afghanistan does not mean the end of our diplomatic and humanitarian mission. https://t.co/uItq4qM8HH
Live now! @StateDeptSpox briefs reporters from the State Department, also streaming on https://t.co/I7ap5Wt0Df. https://t.co/pRTch4bX8I

Food security means having enough safe and nutritious food to support an active, healthy life. The current food crisis can destabilize societies, increase hunger & malnutrition, drive migration & conflict, and cause severe economic dislocation. Learn more:https://t.co/4JjSwhukdX

FACT: Russian forces are plundering and destroying Ukrainian agricultural infrastructure, including silos, farming machinery and fields. This keeps Ukrainian farmers from sowing and planting, harming future harvests and worsening the global food crisis. https://t.co/E9MecLdAs2

Millions more people are at risk of becoming food insecure since President Putin chose to invade Ukraine and systematically capture some of its most productive farmland. https://t.co/xY3NgJGgoY https://t.co/pVr64Cg54E
Russia is creating and spreading false narratives to protect the Kremlin’s image and justify its actions abroad and domestically. Learn the facts: https://t.co/9dAsAKMEG8 https://t.co/A2FKJUqq0U

.@SecBlinken: “Russia’s aggression against Ukraine is a brazen violation of international law, including the @UN Charter, and it’s also contrary to the rules-based international order that we all seek to uphold.” Read his full remarks: https://t.co/B4qogRBp4c https://t.co/bJPlOuz6AR

Have you read the latest on our official blog, DipNote? Check out the newest entries on facing challenges through public-private partnerships, advancing a healthy environment, and protecting life in Zimbabwe through demining efforts. Catch up here: https://t.co/87RfebxJt9

The NPT is the basis for international cooperation to prevent the spread of nuclear weapons and work toward their elimination. The United States remains committed to leading the way on arms control and addressing complex global security challenges. More: https://t.co/Q9THz6UtQl https://t.co/W6AEzC4xuQ

Together with the United Kingdom and France, we’ve released a set of principles and best practices that should be expected of every responsible NPT nuclear weapon state, among them that every effort has to be made to ensure that nuclear weapons are not used again. https://t.co/XmZNOltuBn

The @WhiteHouse has unveiled a new U.S. Strategy Toward Sub-Saharan Africa, articulating our vision for a 21st Century U.S.-African Partnership. It recognizes the tremendous, positive opportunities that exist to advance shared interests. More: https://t.co/DrIlMPFxxU

.@SecBlinken on the U.S. strategy for Sub-Saharan Africa: "Our strategy is rooted in the recognition that sub-Saharan Africa is a major geopolitical force–one that has shaped our past, is shaping our present, and will shape our future." More from his trip: https://t.co/tskAIg7ajP https://t.co/BoyMpByjzk

.@SecBlinken: The overwhelming majority of people across Africa prefer democracy to any other form of government. Even greater majorities oppose the authoritarian alternatives to democracy: more than 70% reject military rule and more than 80% reject one-man rule. https://t.co/UCEkscX9jQ

.@ProsperAfricaUS is a U.S. government initiative that supports two-way trade and investment between the U.S. and Africa by connecting buyers, suppliers and investors. Read more on how they are contributing to countries across Africa: https://t.co/SEmWiTQnDZ

In "The Week at State," @POTUS signs the U.S. Instruments of Ratification of the Accession Protocols to the North Atlantic Treaty for Finland and Sweden, and @SecBlinken travels to Cambodia, the Philippines, South Africa, the DRC & Rwanda. Visit https://t.co/58Uc9lIgDz for more. https://t.co/A95GeUE0hJ

.@SecBlinken: "The United States will not dictate Africa’s choices, and neither should anyone else. The right to make these choices belongs to Africans, and Africans alone." More in our latest @ShareAmerica article: https://t.co/menckSPe9G https://t.co/2VDzaZMcoA

The U.S. recognizes that Sub-Saharan Africa plays a critical role in advancing global priorities to the benefit of Africans and Americans. That is why we are building on our actions and commitments to deepen our engagement and partnerships in Africa. More: https://t.co/DrIlMPnWGm https://t.co/Zvjs2zb3ng

In our latest DipNote, @scidiplomacyusa A/S Medina shares how we recently joined hands to raise the world's ambition to support a right to a clean, healthy, and sustainable environment. Together we will continue the vital work of developing this right. https://t.co/ySaKU4CYZX
The United States has held firm to our longstanding key objectives: preserving peace and stability in the Taiwan Strait and the broader Indo-Pacific region. https://t.co/Jag832FKOB

.@StateDeptPM works with partners around the world to save lives and promote opportunity by clearing deadly #landmines #UXO, returning land to productive use. Learn more about these efforts in Zimbabwe’s CORSAN minefield & Sengwe Wildlife Corridor. https://t.co/ofjeqwJlEZ

As the biggest land mammal on Earth, elephants have the largest brains and are social and emotional creatures. Elephants are also endangered and face threats from poaching and habitat loss. The U.S. is committed to protecting elephants from wildlife trafficking. #WorldElephantDay https://t.co/vWNYhYtBzx

Want an inside look at diplomacy in action but never thought you could afford to? The State Department is now offering paid student internships. Learn more: https://t.co/Tj0Wq6Xwc4. https://t.co/a82zmH029c

Through programs like @NSLIY, @YesProgramNews, @FLEXProgram, and more, we are working with young leaders to build stronger ties between nations and empower the next generation. On #YouthDay, visit https://t.co/VXIEo3ac0F to find your next global experience! https://t.co/TgPR6WohZP

As more people are vaccinated world-wide, including children, we are thankful for countries partnering with the U.S. to get shots in arms. To continue our partnerships, the U.S., through #COVAX, donated nearly 1.5 million additional Pfizer COVID-19 pediatric doses to Bangladesh. https://t.co/enxQKM7YvM

In today's press briefing, @StateDeputySpox took questions on how the U.S. is responding to Putin’s war of choice, Austin Tice and his 10 years of captivity in Syria, and nuclear tests in the DPRK. Read the full transcript: https://t.co/djEQVNmYsu

The Academy for Women Entrepreneurs (AWE) was launched in 2019 by @ECAatState and has empowered 16,000+ enterprising women in 80 countries. Read how one AWE alumna, Umunyana Marie Chantal, helps provide access to maternal health information in Rwanda. https://t.co/WKUEppYQQP.

.@SecBlinken: "We have the deepest admiration for the people of Rwanda, their courage, their resilience, all that they have built. We hope the conversations we’ve had today will help our partnership grow even stronger." More from the press avail in Rwanda: https://t.co/SAUzTlCvC3
The U.S. has provided $1 billion toward the $2 billion commitment to African countries for emergency food security assistance. Read more in the fact sheet: https://t.co/ToL9g1U7D7.

98 countries, including 15 @g20org members, have signed onto the Roadmap for Global Food Security, affirming their commitment to act with urgency to respond to the urgent food security and nutrition needs of millions of vulnerable people around the world. https://t.co/CvRnPGpNpc https://t.co/kAVXOMRmDI

At the G7 Summit in June, @POTUS and G7 leaders announced over $4.5 billion to address global food security, more than half from the U.S. The $2.76 billion in U.S. government funding is helping to protect the world’s most vulnerable populations in Africa. https://t.co/YOIopvQrtb
